claim: Independent cross-check confirms the explorer's claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch implements provider-specific configuration isolation and boundary enforcement to prevent drift between Claude, Codex, and custom provider assumptions in evidence mode.

evidence.summary: Cross-check verified: Provider contract normalization exists with distinct capabilities for Claude (no sandbox, permission-mode, budget enforcement), Codex (sandbox modes, file_write shell_exec controls), and custom (external/local modes, shell execution).
evidence.why: Direct read confirms explorer's line range and summary are accurate.

evidence.summary: Cross-check verified: buildProviderInvocation generates provider-specific CLI arguments. Lines 77-102 show Claude uses -p, --model, --permission-mode, --max-budget-usd, --no-session-persistence. Lines 43-60 show Codex uses exec, --cd, --sandbox, --output-last-message.
evidence.why: Direct read confirms provider-specific invocation isolation.

evidence.summary: Cross-check verified: executionConfig at lines 442-444 clears Claude model when provider is Codex. Lines 445-458 set Claude-specific agent_tool_profile and clear tools/settings. Line 460 sets custom_sandbox boundary mode.
evidence.why: Direct read confirms configuration normalization happens as claimed.

evidence.summary: Cross-check verified: buildBoundarySnapshot at lines 99-107 uses git ls-files or filesystem walk, skips allowed roots, captures file digests. diffBoundarySnapshots at lines 110-120 compares before/after for mutations.
evidence.why: Direct read confirms the boundary snapshot system implementation.

evidence.summary: Cross-check verified: Runtime flow captures pre-execution snapshot (line 511), runs agents (lines 514-560), captures post-execution snapshot (line 561), compares violations (line 562), injects blockers into readiness.
evidence.why: Direct read confirms the enforcement mechanism works as claimed.

evidence.summary: Cross-check verified: parseJsonCandidate (lines 326-341) repairs line ranges (line 331), quoted keys with spaces (line 332), trailing commas (line 333). extractJsonObject (lines 343-363) handles fenced code blocks (lines 349-354) and object extraction (lines 355-361).
evidence.why: Direct read confirms JSON repair handles provider output variations.

evidence.summary: Cross-check verified: sanitizeMissing (lines 142-148) filters out status phrases like 'no missing data' using isNonMissingStatusItem (lines 132-140) which matches patterns like 'no missing', 'all complete', '100%'.
evidence.why: Direct read confirms missing sanitization prevents status text pollution.

evidence.summary: Cross-check verified: evidenceRecordScope (lines 382-406) normalizes various field names (scope, evidence_scope, source_scope, kind, type) to canonical values (project, external, user, unknown).
evidence.why: Direct read confirms scope normalization handles provider field naming variations.

evidence.summary: Cross-check verified: projectEvidenceBlockingIssues (lines 513-530) enforces that project-scoped evidence must have repo-relative path (lines 415-418), blocking claims without concrete locators.
evidence.why: Direct read confirms project evidence path enforcement.

evidence.summary: Cross-check verified: Test 'codex dispatch does not inherit a claude default model or force read-only sandbox' asserts provider isolation (line 198: config.ai.provider is codex; line 201: model doesn't contain 'claude').
evidence.why: Direct read confirms test evidence exists as claimed.

evidence.summary: Cross-check verified: Test 'claude dispatch does not pass tool restriction flags and relies on boundary enforcement' asserts Claude receives empty settings/tools strings (lines 236-240) and sets no_session_persistence (line 241).
evidence.why: Direct read confirms prompt-based boundary evidence exists.

evidence.summary: Cross-check verified: Test 'boundary blocks project file mutation even when agent results pass' simulates mutation (line 381), verifies status blocked (line 394), and checks BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blocker (line 396).
evidence.why: Direct read confirms boundary enforcement test evidence.

evidence.summary: Cross-check verified: Doctrine confirms Claude dispatch doesn't use tool restriction flags; agent_tool_profile expresses semantic mode; safety relies on prompt contract, artifact root, boundary snapshots, and readiness blockers.
evidence.why: Direct read confirms design documentation matches security model.

risks: Git-based boundary detection at lines 99-107 of evidence-dispatch.ts uses git ls-files first; if the repo has uncommitted changes that are not part of agent actions, they could be incorrectly attributed to the agent or mask actual violations.
JSON repair logic at lines 330-333 of evidence-dispatch.ts uses regex transformations that could incorrectly normalize malformed but semantically valid JSON from an unfamiliar provider pattern.
No evidence that Codex or custom providers have been tested with real agent execution in demand evidence context; all tests use mocked spawnProviderPrompt callbacks.
missing: No evidence of stress testing the boundary system against agents that deliberately attempt to mutate project files beyond the single simulated mutation in test line 381.
No evidence of how the system handles provider timeout or crash scenarios during evidence dispatch; lines 526-536 show error handling but no test coverage.
No evidence of cross-provider drift testing where an agent assumes a different provider's capabilities during execution.

result.notes: All 13 evidence items independently verified against source files. Line ranges, summaries, and cross-references are accurate. The implementation matches the claimed security model. Risks are mitigated by boundary snapshot system and readiness gate. Missing evidence represents test coverage gaps but not architectural defects.
